_doc: === ANALYSIS STATUS ENTRY: Tracks which inference job has run for this narrative + lock state ===
_doc_purpose: The load-bearing reason this layer exists: a re-imported archive that omitted analysis_status would lose the `Source: Skill, locked: true` rows that protect skill-attested analyses from being silently re-run by a subsequent bulk-analysis pass. With this layer present, locks survive the round-trip.
_narrative_id: REQUIRED. Rewritten on import to match the imported narrative slug.
_job_type: REQUIRED. InferenceJobType variant name (e.g. ArcClassification, CharacterArc, SceneSequel, Focalization, SubplotDetection, CommitmentDetection, DramaticIrony, DisinfoFingerprint, ClaimOrigin, CounterNarrative, MotivationInference). Payload-carrying variants are stored as the bare variant name — payload is NOT round-tripped (jobs are re-runnable).
_scope: REQUIRED. "story" for narrative-wide; "actor:<uuid>" / "arc:<uuid>" / "chapter:<index>" for scoped analyses (e.g. ActorArcClassification has one row per Actor entity, scoped as "actor:<entity_uuid>").
_source: REQUIRED. One of: Auto | Skill | Manual. Auto = server worker pool wrote this row after a job completed. Skill = an external skill (e.g. /tensa-narrative-llm) attested this result. Manual = a human entered or attested it. Skill / Manual default to locked: true; Auto defaults to locked: false.
_skill: OPTIONAL. Skill name when source=Skill, otherwise null.
_model: OPTIONAL. Model identifier the analysis was produced with.
_locked: REQUIRED. When true, bulk-analysis runs MUST skip this entry unless force=true. Defaults: Skill / Manual → true, Auto → false.
summary: Cinderella arc detected — protagonist undergoes upward fortune trajectory inflected by external coercion.
_confidence: OPTIONAL. Confidence in [0.0, 1.0] the producer attached.
_result_refs: OPTIONAL. Array of ResultRef pointers to the actual writes (kind: annotation | pinned_fact | situation_update | revision | inference_result | ...). Lets Studio render deep links from the Analyses tab.
